A. Ottinger and c. Twitchell, tick
ct brokers, of 612 Riverside avenue.
against the city of Spokane and Le-
roy Waller, chief of police, is a case
that is being argued at some length
today in Judge Kennans court. Alex
NJ. Winston, representing the plain
tins, laid great stress upon the point
of license raised in the city ordi-
nance covering the question. lie con-
tented that a private citizen was en-
titled to sell railroad tickets as were
railroad agents. They are brokers
in each sense. Winston claimed IS
license would be a fair tax and that
railroad agents should also share
therein. lie quoted at length upon
the supreme courts decision in sew
eral states as being unconstitutional
and In his opinion the ordinance could
not stand.


1t is claimed by the plaintiffs that
the scalpers ordinance was deal
put up by the railroads and that the
city council went a step further in
